publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-18 23:24:51 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Film scripts and pre-production material; photographs, stills with relevant negatives and film slides; publicity material, drafts and final copies of articles, newspaper and magazine clippings; personal and professional correspondence; books with inscriptions, postcards, handwritten notes, and other ephemera of Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. (May 27, 1943 - July 29, 1987), one of the pioneers of independent gay cinema in the 1970s and '80s, documenting his personal and professional activity, from 1966 to 1987.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. papers document the professional and personal activity of the film director, writer, documentarian, and producer of independent gay cinema, Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. (May 27, 1943 - July 29, 1987), dating from 1966 to 1987.

The collection contains film scripts, screenplays, and screen treatments with several notes; pre-production material, such as documents related to film castings, budget, copyrights, and other legal documents; photographs and film stills; relevant negatives and film slides; publicity material, such as flyers, ad sheets, press releases, press kits, and theater tickets; drafts and final copies of Bressan's articles, public reactions to his films, including Buddies (1985), Abuse (1983), Thank You, Mr. President (1983), and Juice (1983), newspaper and magazine clippings; personal and professional correspondence, books with inscriptions, postcards, handwritten notes, and other ephemera.

The collection includes personal correspondence, such as letters, postcards, and handwritten notes from several people, including Robert Woods, and friends. The folder Harvey Milk includes correspondence from Harvey Milk to Bressan in September 1977 and a few protest documents related to Milk's assassination in 1978. Under the Biographical Files, the sub-series titled Frank Capra indicates Bressan's affiliation to Capra's work, including correspondence from and about Frank Capra, a typed script of the film "Meet John Doe" (1941) with a personal inscription by the director to Arthur J. Bressan Jr., and several drafts of Bressan's article on Frank Capra.

Apart from scripts and pre-production material of Bressan's well-known films, such as the films Abuse (1983), Juice (1984), Passing strangers (1974), the documentary Gay USA (1977), and others, there are several unreleased scripts, screenplays, and proposals. Other writings include the novelization of the Buddies by Michael Mannion, Bressan's college paper "My Kinsman, Major Molineux," and a memorial article by the American LGBT activist, film historian, author, and good friend of Bressan, Vito Russo (1987). The collection does not contain film reels or any audiovisual material.

archdesc.bioghist:
Biographical / Historical
Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. (May 27, 1943 - July 29, 1987) was a film director, writer, documentarian, and producer of independent gay cinema. He was born and raised on West 68th Street in the Lincoln Square area of Manhattan, New York City. Educated in public and then parochial schools (Iona College), he completed his Master of Art in American Civilization, focused on the "Cinema of Frank Capra" at New York University. He became a high school teacher and then a Federal Director/ Administrator of Education. While teaching high school sophomores in a New York City ghetto, he got involved in filming, renting a Super 8 camera, and having his students create their films. Soon after, the 25 years-old Bressan left academia and focused on making films.
His first film was a silent Super 8 short (18min., black and white) titled Boys in 1969. The same year, he moved to San Francisco, California, and participated in the first San Francisco Gay Film Festival with his short film. In 1971 he worked in the film industry as an editor in the American Indian Alcatraz Trial Defense Film. A year later, he created the documentary short Coming out (16mm, 10mm, color), which participated in the first official San Francisco Pride march in 1972. Passing strangers (1974) was his first featured film, which earned him the Best Director Award at the 1974 San Francisco Erotic Film Festival. In 1977, Bressan filmed the featured documentary Gay USA, showcasing LGBT Pride celebrations across the country during Anita Bryant's anti-gay crusade. Bressan's last project, Buddies(1985), was the first featured film about AIDS and Bressan's most well-known film. Other films include Forbidden Letters (1979), <Family Affair (1982), Thank You, Mr. President (1983), Abuse (1983), Pleasure Beach (1983), Juice (1984) and Daddy dearest (1984).
Bressan died of AIDS in 1987.
Filmography Boys (short, 1969) Coming out (documentary short, 1972) Passing Strangers (1974) Gay USA (documentary, 1977) Forbidden Letters (1979) Family Affair (1982) Abuse (1983) Pleasure Beach (1983) Thank You, Mr. President (1983) Juice (1984) Daddy dearest (1984) Buddies (1985)

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. papers, #7892. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Based on "The Backstory on the Films and Papers of Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. and How We Got To Where We Are Today on The Bressan Project", By Jenni Olson (March 2019)

A year or two after Arthur J. Bressan Jr died in 1987, his sister, Roe (Rose Marie) Bressan, donated all his papers and film elements to Frameline in San Francisco. The collection (unprocessed) was held at Frameline's storage space at Iron Mountain. In 2002, Frameline changed offices, and it was decided that all the materials (the papers and film elements) would be given to the San Francisco Media Archive (the non-profit arm of Oddball Films). Roe Bressan had also moved, and Frameline could no longer reach her. SFMA kept the collection safe without processing or preserving it.

Jenni Olson (LGBT film historian and archivist), a former staff at Frameline, maintained contact with Frameline and the SFMA/Oddball. In the 1990s and 2000s, Olson had been in touch with festivals interested in Arthur's films. She also had tried unsuccessfully to re-establish contact with Arthur's sister. In 2016, Olson found Joe Rubin, one of the founders of the indie distributor Vinegar Syndrome, who had come into possession of the original 16mm negative of Arthur's film Buddies (1985). Despite their discussions, they couldn't preserve or use the material as they couldn't find the right holder or estate. Around this time, Frameline deposited the film elements (not donated) at UCLA Film & Television Archive for safe keeping (and later for preservation). UCLA finally preserved Bressan's films. In December 2017, Jenni managed to find Rose Marie Bressan. Together, they created the Bressan Project and coordinated efforts to restore and re-release all of the films of Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. In March 2019, Jenni Olson contacted RMC Human Sexuality Curator Brenda J. Marston, asking for a home for the papers of Arthur J. Bressan. In January 2020, 3 cubic feet of Arthur's J. Bressan, Jr. papers were purchased and accessioned by RMC.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Bressan's biography and filmography note by Steven Riskin. Bressan's astrological chart; a hotel card from London, a gym card from Honolulu, Hawai (1980).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards, handwritten notes, accompanying personal photographs, a single letter from Robert Woods, a mirrored letter from Bressan's friend Ellen (1970), and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains health information from the period (1972-1974).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Protected Health Information (PHI). Restricted until 2037 due to HIPAA Privacy Rule.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter from Harvey Milk (9/29/1977), a folded protest flyer titled "Who killed Harvey Milk, a comic with the title "The Wonderful World of Dan White," and "a booklet titled "I promise you, a collection of poems for Harvey Milk."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Arthur J. Bressan's photograph at a young age and a memorial article by Vito Russo (1946– 1990).

did.unittitle: The Great Singers, from the dawn of opera to our own time by Henry Pleasants

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book with an inscription with Christman wishes from Larraine to Arthur J. Bressan Jr. in 1966. Simon and Schuster New York, 1966.

did.unittitle: "Leading Boys, directed by Frank Capra" article by Arthur Bressan and drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Directing Men Section of Connection Magazine. Several article drafts with a different title ("The Boys World of Frank Capra") and corrections by A.J. Bressan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an interview from William Hornbeck, the editor executive for Universal Pictures. Correspondence from Frank Capra.

did.unittitle: Meet John Doe. Typed script inscribed by Frank Capra to Arthur J. Bressan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a program from the conference "Meet Frank Capra x 446" at the University of California, Berkeley (June 15 to 17). The film "Meet John Doe", was directed by Frank Capra in 1941.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Frank Capra, several films and protagonists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a publicity booklet of Frank Capra's film "State of the Union" (1948).

did.unittitle: Series II. Scripts and pre-production material

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by film title when possible.

did.unittitle: Abuse. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four drafts with handwritten notes and corrections.

did.unittitle: Abuse. Audition material and pre-production notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the weekly shooting-schedule.

did.unittitle: Abuse. Copies of invoices and relevant correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invoices by several film production companies. Handwritten notes on contacts (names and addresses). Film Workshop of Westchester, Kin-O-Lux, inc.

did.unittitle: Abuse. Dialogue transcript of the Arthur J. Bressan, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Steven R. Mc Millin Production. 3 copies.

did.unittitle: American. An original screen treatment by Arthur J. Bressan Jr. and Martin Poriss
Boy in the Window. An original script by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
Buddies. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
Burial for the Dead. A film treatment by Arthur J. Bressan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the original screenplay by Richard Klingerman. First draft.

did.unittitle: Divine Rites. A screen treatment by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
"…For we are Everywhere." The Harvey Milk Story. An original screen treatment by Arthur J. Bressan Jr. and Randy Alfred

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relevant Material Box 1, Folder 3.

did.unittitle: Gay USA. Copyrights and other legal documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: U.S. Copyright Office, Library of Congress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to publicity, and budget.

did.unittitle: Gay USA. Making-of, audience review comments, and a transcript
Homecoming. An original film treatment by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
Inside Norma Garland. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes scripts in two versions with handwritten notes and a draft. Unreleased screenplay.

did.unittitle: Juice. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: Minor Obsession. An original shooting script by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
My Night at Norma's. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies.

did.unittitle: My Night at Norma's. Shooting schedule
A night prayer. A suggestion for a screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second draft.

did.unittitle: Pleasure Beach. An original screenplay by Richard Lawrence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts with handwritten notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enrico Caruso's discography, Artie's discography, and more.

did.unittitle: Rape. And The Last Session. Handwritten notes on two scripts
Seven Years and a Day. A screen transliteration by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.
Song of Kristal Nacht. A play with music by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dedicated to Gerda Maria Goddard. Includes handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: South-By-Southeast. Original screenplay by Jerry Douglas, adaptation by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes budget, the plot, information about the production, and more—uncompleted film.

did.unittitle: The Letters. A playscript by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two versions with handwritten notes. One copy has a note "Edited and with an Introduction by J. Michael Mannion".

did.unittitle: The Making of Edward Porter. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten script notes on a legal pad and typed scripts.

did.unittitle: The old Man. A screen treatment by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original screenplay.

did.unittitle: The Rushes. A playscript by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: Untitled scripts, proposals, and other writings by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Aids is people," a proposal; A college paper titled, "My Kinsman, Major Molineux", a study in personal and political initiation; "Requiem for a Gadfly" and more. There is also a selection of poems from several poets and a dialogue poem by Arthur J. Bressan. Includes handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: Unwritten Rules. An original screenplay by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten notes. Two drafts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by film title when possible.

did.unittitle: III.A. Prints, Negatives and Contact Sheets
Abuse. Contact sheets and photographs of battered kids with negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Still frames with associated negatives. Content statement: This folder contains images of child physical abuse.

did.unittitle: Abuse. Studio photographs in black and white

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio shooting photographs of Raphael Sbarge, Richard Ryder, and other protagonists. Includes a few photocopies.

did.unittitle: Abuse. The Castro Theater marquee in black and white

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Night photograph. San Francisco (SF) International Film Festival presented Arthur Bressan's film "Abuse" in Castro Theater (SF).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Still frames.

did.unittitle: Daddy Dearest. Film stills and un-reproduced stills
Daddy Dearest. Film stills with negatives and other photographs
Film premieres, ceremonies, theaters, actors, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs related to the films: Juice, Daddy Dearest, Buddies, and Pleasure Beach.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Removed from a photographic album because of its pure condition.

did.unittitle: Forbidden Letters. Contact sheets and stills (with negatives)
Forbidden Letters. Contact sheets and stills (with negatives)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Still frames. Black & white

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Behind-the-scenes and the premiere; casting, and other photographs (with negatives)

did.unittitle: Passing Strangers. Contact sheets with negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Still frames.

did.unittitle: Passing Strangers. Film stills and photocollage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocollage with the poet Arthur Rimbaud.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 mm slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 mm slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyers, ad sheets, press releases, articles, one-sheets, press kits, and theater tickets. Folders include publicity-related photographs and negatives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by film title when possible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of the protagonists (Richard Ryder, Raphel Sbarge, Steve James).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material in German. Cornell Cinema Program "Gay People at Cornell." Invitation by Vito Russo.

did.unittitle: Buddies. A novel by Michael Mannion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles in German.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Festival Finalist Certification, the American Film and Video Festival, Educational Film Library Association. GPAA Awards. There is material in French. Correspondence by the San Francisco State University, the International Cay and Lesbian Archives, the Alliance for Gay and Lesbian Artists.

did.unittitle: Buddies. Legal-size poster and Arthur J. Bressan's interview

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pressbook in German.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ad sheets with relevant negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: International Film festival Berlin, Film Fest Journal (1983) in German and English; and 4. schwules filmwochenende würzburg program (1985); Cinemateket-Filmkluben (August 1983); Frameline, 8th San Francisco International Lesbian and Gay Film, Chicago Film Makers (1985); In the pink (1986); Hauptstadt! Film Festival Special (1983) and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several add sheets with the associated negatives. Color 8x10 photographs of Robert Adams (Larry) in a cell and Richard Locke (Richard) in a cell.

did.unittitle: Gay USA. Clippings and festival participation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an open letter from the makers of the film.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ad sheets with associated negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bressan's interview by David Lamble of Coming up! Newspaper; "Crimes Against Passion, an interview with Gay Screenwriter Barry Sandler," by Arthur J. Bressan Jr. in Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs with the associated negatives.

did.unittitle: Juice. Publicity material, Castro Theater (San Francisco).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent by Frameline (a media arts nonprofit dedicated to queer cinema connecting filmmakers and audiences in the Bay Area and worldwide). Includes invoices, budget information, and a handwritten note by Arthur J. Bressan Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Home Video (January 1982); Stallion with Arthur J. Bressan interview (April 1984); Coming Out with Arthur J. Bressan interview (August 1984), After Dark (September 1985); USA Today (October 1985); Gay Scene, vol 15, no. 3. August 1984; Win, December 19, 1974; The Advocate (July 1974); The Village Voice, July 1974.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to Abuse, Daddy Dearest and Bressan's interviews.

did.unittitle: Passing Strangers. Promotional material, screenings in Sweden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material in the Swedish language. Contact Sheets associated with negatives are in this folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McNamara, Tom. "Gay Sex Film is an Honest Portrayal." The San Francisco Phoenix, vol.2, No.26. 11 October 1974. Fairbanks, Harold. "Just Passing Strangers? There's more to a classified as than just fats, fems, and dopers". The Advocate, issue 143, 31 July 1974, and more articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ad sheets with relevant negatives and stills.

did.unittitle: Pleasure Beach. 1985 Erotic Awards and promotional material

unittitle.i: Thank You, Mr. President: The Press Conferences of JFK
